LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. AFTER THE HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON PLAINTIFF EVRA CONSTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-16-556205 WITH CASE CGC-15-549556 AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF EVA CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE THE CONSOLIDATED CASES UNDER THE LEAD CASE 549556 WITH CASE 556205 FOR ALL PURPOSES IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE RIGHT OF THE OWNER PARTIES TO ASK THE TRIAL JUDGE TO SEVER CASE 556205 FOR TRIAL OR BIFURCATE THE TRIAL TO AVOID ANY PREJUDICE TO THE OWNER PARTIES. THE CASES ARE CONSOLIDATED FOR ALL PURPOSES. THE LEAD CASE IS 549556 AND ALL FURTHER FILINGS MUST BE IN THAT CASE ONLY. THE TRIAL DATE OF JANUARY 29, 2018 IN 556205 IS VACATED AND THE TRIAL DATE OF MARCH 12, 2018 IN CASE 549556 NOW APPLIES TO ALL THREE CONSOLIDATED CASES. CONSOLIDATION WILL FACILITATE JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND POSSIBLY AVOID INCONSISTENT RULINGS BY COORDINATING DISCOVERY, ADVANCING SETTLEMENT PROSPECTS, POSSIBLY ELIMINATING THE NEED FOR WITNESSES TO TESTIFY TWICE, AND ALLOWING THE PARTIES TO OBTAIN RULINGS ON COLLATERAL SOURCE RULE AND ASSERTED DOUBLE RECOVERY THAT ARE APPLICABLE TO BOTH 549556 AND 556205. NO PREJUDICE WILL RESULT TO THE OWNER DEFENDANTS SINCE THEY WILL BE ABLE TO SEEK SEVERANCE OR BIFURCATION FROM THE TRIAL JUDGE TO ADDRESS THEIR CONCERNS ABOUT THE JURY DECIDING THE DEFECT CLAIMS LEARNING ABOUT THEIR INSURANCE COVERAGE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MITCH L. LYON, CSR# 6840

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-16-556205 WITH CASE CGC-15-549556
Off Calendar Aug-04-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jordan Rodman, Esq. of Sims, Lawrence & Arruti, Tel. 916.797.8881, appearing for plaintiff and moving party Evra Construction, Inc.;
Drew T. Williams, Esq. of McNamara, Ney, Beatty, Slattery, Borges & Ambacher LLP, Tel. 925.939.5330, appearing for cross complainant/cross defendant Quality Painting & Construction;
Brian M. Junginer, Esq. of McInerney & Dillon, Tel. 510.465.7100, for plaintiffs (in consolidated case) and opposing party Casa Dolores Association, et al.;
Michael Martinovsky, Esq. of the Law Office of Michael Martinovsky, Tel. 415.230.5360, for defendant/cross complainant Quality Painting & Construction, via Court Call;
Mena Arsalai, Esq. of Hansen, Kohls, Sommer & Jacob LLP, Tel. 916.781.2550, for interested party Farmers Insurance, via Court Call.

RULING: The matter was discussed. After the hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF EVRA CONSTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-16-556205 WITH CASE CGC-15-549556 as follows: Plaintiff Eva Construction, Inc.'s motion to consolidate the consolidated cases under the lead case 549556 with case 556205 for all purposes is granted without prejudice to the right of the owner parties to ask the trial judge to sever case 556205 for trial or bifurcate the trial to avoid any prejudice to the owner parties. The cases are consolidated for all purposes. The lead case is 549556 and all further filings must be in that case only. The trial date of January 29, 2018 in 556205 is vacated and the trial date of March 12, 2018 in case 549556 now applies to all three consolidated cases. Consolidation will facilitate judicial economy and possibly avoid inconsistent rulings by coordinating discovery, advancing settlement prospects, possibly eliminating the need for witnesses to testify twice, and allowing the parties to obtain rulings on collateral source rule and asserted double recovery that are applicable to both 549556 and 556205. No prejudice will result to the owner defendants since they will be able to seek severance or bifurcation from the trial judge to address their concerns about the jury deciding the defect claims learning about their insurance coverage. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR#
6840
